base.name: Purdue University

englishRequirement.note: English test (TOEFL 80+, IELTS 6.5+, or Duolingo 105+) required for international students. English Composition is recommended to reach 24 credits but does NOT waive the English test requirement.

base.notes: All engineering programs require minimum 3.0 GPA and completion of 9 core first-year engineering courses. Transfer applicants from outside pathway/articulation programs are reviewed on a space-available basis. In-progress courses are accepted, but final grades may be required before an admissions decision. Source: admissions.purdue.edu/become-student/transfer/engineering-transfer-criteria/

majors.name: Aeronautical & Astronautical Engineering
majors.note: RESTRICTED — open to articulation pathway programs and regional campus transfers only. Outside transfer applicants are not admitted directly.

majors.name: Biomedical Engineering
majors.note: Science selective must be one of: Chemistry II (CHM 11610/11620/11630), CS 15900, CS 18000, or CS 17600.

majors.name: Computer Engineering
majors.note: Chemistry I is NOT required — another physics or math course may substitute. Physics II is already listed as required and satisfies this substitution.

majors.name: Electrical Engineering
majors.note: Chemistry I is NOT required — another physics or math course may substitute. Physics II is already listed as required and satisfies this substitution.

majors.name: Environmental & Ecological Engineering
majors.note: Science selective MUST be Chemistry II (CHM 11610/11620/11630). General advanced science/math/physics is not accepted for this major.

majors.name: Mechanical Engineering
majors.note: RESTRICTED at West Lafayette — open to articulation pathway programs and regional campus transfers only. The Indianapolis campus is open to all qualified transfer applicants.

majors.name: Motorsports Engineering
majors.note: RESTRICTED — open to articulation pathway programs and regional campus transfers only.

majors.name: Interdisciplinary Engineering (IDES)
majors.note: ENGR 13100 and ENGR 13200 (Engineering Problem Solving and Engineering Projects) are specifically required and cannot be waived. Must also submit a one-page statement of interest.
